sections.paragraph:
ἐγκεράννυμι pf. pass. ptc. ἐγκεκραμένος (Hom. et al.; Ps.-Lucian, Amor. 32) to combine w. another, mix, pass. be united τινί w. someone IEph 5:1.—DELG s.v. κεράννυμι.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 273
